evals.name: app-of-apps-audit
evals.prompt: Audit the Argo CD GitOps repository at tests/argo-repo-audit/app-of-apps/ using the argo-repo-audit skill.
expectations: Identifies the app-of-apps pattern
Detects two environments: staging and production
Counts Application resources (should find approximately 9)
Counts AppProject resources (should find 2)
Identifies sync policy configuration per Application (which have automated sync, which don't)
Checks AppProject sourceRepos — identifies whether wildcards are used
Checks AppProject destinations — identifies whether wildcards are used
Checks for ignoreDifferences configuration on Applications that need it
Checks for retry configuration on sync policies
Identifies sync wave ordering via annotations
Produces a structured report with Summary, Best Practices, Security, and Recommendations
Assigns an overall status (PASS, WARN, or FAIL) based on findings

evals.name: applicationset-audit
evals.prompt: Audit the Argo CD GitOps repository at tests/argo-repo-audit/applicationset/ using the argo-repo-audit skill.
expectations: Identifies the ApplicationSet pattern
Identifies generator types used (git directory, cluster, matrix)
Checks whether goTemplate is enabled vs fasttemplate
Checks for progressive sync (rollingSync) configuration
Checks for preserveResourcesOnDeletion setting
Evaluates template completeness (destination, source, syncPolicy)
Checks for goTemplateOptions (missingkey=error)
Identifies that ApplicationSets deploy to the default project — flags if concerning
Checks whether prune is enabled in generated Applications' sync policy
Produces a structured report with ApplicationSet-specific findings
Includes ApplicationSet-specific recommendations

evals.name: mixed-issues-audit
evals.prompt: Audit the Argo CD GitOps repository at tests/argo-repo-audit/mixed-issues/ using the argo-repo-audit skill.
expectations: Finds Application, AppProject, Rollout, AnalysisTemplate, Workflow, and WorkflowTemplate resources
Flags the AppProject with wildcard (*) in sourceRepos as Critical security issue
Flags the AppProject with wildcard (*) in destinations as Critical security issue
Flags wildcard clusterResourceWhitelist ('*/*') as Critical
Detects plain-text Secret (cluster-secret.yaml or plain-secret.yaml) as Critical security issue
Detects hardcoded password in redis Application helm values
Flags Applications with no syncPolicy (api-server.yaml) as missing automated sync
Flags Application with prune: false (frontend.yaml) as incomplete sync config
Flags Application using targetRevision: HEAD as risky for production
Checks Rollout for AnalysisTemplate with both successCondition and failureCondition
Checks Workflow for missing activeDeadlineSeconds and resource limits
Reports overall status as FAIL due to Critical security issues
Recommendations include at least 3 Critical-severity items

evals.name: validate-only
evals.prompt: Run only manifest validation on tests/argo-repo-audit/app-of-apps/. Just check YAML syntax and schema validity, do not perform a full audit.
expectations: Performs YAML syntax validation
Performs schema validation against Argo CRDs
Does NOT produce a full audit report with best practices and security
Reports validation pass/fail result
Handles Application and AppProject resources correctly
Reports summary of files checked

evals.name: detect-wildcard-project
evals.prompt: Audit the Argo CD GitOps repository at tests/argo-repo-audit/targeted-bugs/wildcard-project/ using the argo-repo-audit skill. This repo has one AppProject and one Application.
expectations: Flags sourceRepos: ['*'] as Critical security issue
Flags destinations with server: '*' and namespace: '*' as Critical
Flags clusterResourceWhitelist with group: '*' kind: '*' as Critical
Reports overall status as FAIL due to Critical security findings
Does NOT flag the Application — it has proper syncPolicy, pinned targetRevision, and retry config

evals.name: detect-plain-secret
evals.prompt: Audit the Argo CD GitOps repository at tests/argo-repo-audit/targeted-bugs/plain-secret/ using the argo-repo-audit skill. This repo has one Application and one plain-text Secret.
expectations: Detects kind: Secret (db-credentials.yaml) as Critical security issue — plain-text secret committed to Git
Recommends using SealedSecret, ExternalSecret, SOPS, or Vault instead
Reports overall status as FAIL due to Critical security finding
Does NOT flag the Application itself — it has proper syncPolicy

evals.name: detect-no-sync-policy
evals.prompt: Audit the Argo CD GitOps repository at tests/argo-repo-audit/targeted-bugs/no-sync-policy/ using the argo-repo-audit skill. This repo has one Application with no syncPolicy.
expectations: Flags the Application as missing syncPolicy (no automated sync, no prune, no selfHeal)
Recommends adding syncPolicy with automated, prune, selfHeal, and retry
Severity is Warning (not Critical — missing sync is operational risk, not security vulnerability)
Does NOT produce false positives on other aspects of this Application

evals.name: detect-head-in-prod
evals.prompt: Audit the Argo CD GitOps repository at tests/argo-repo-audit/targeted-bugs/head-in-prod/ using the argo-repo-audit skill. This repo has one Application targeting production with targetRevision: HEAD.
expectations: Flags targetRevision: HEAD as risky for a production Application
Explains the risk: HEAD means any commit to the default branch is immediately synced to production without review
Recommends pinning to a specific tag, commit SHA, or release branch
Severity is Warning (not Critical — it's a best practice violation, not a security vulnerability)

evals.name: detect-weak-analysis
evals.prompt: Audit the Argo CD GitOps repository at tests/argo-repo-audit/targeted-bugs/weak-analysis/ using the argo-repo-audit skill. This repo has a Rollout with a canary strategy and an AnalysisTemplate.
expectations: Identifies the AnalysisTemplate has successCondition but no failureCondition
Explains the risk: without failureCondition, the analysis may not fail fast enough on degraded metrics
Recommends adding a failureCondition (e.g., result[0] < threshold) and failureLimit
Does NOT flag the Rollout's canary steps as problematic — the step configuration itself is valid

evals.name: detect-no-deadline-workflow
evals.prompt: Audit the Argo CD GitOps repository at tests/argo-repo-audit/targeted-bugs/no-deadline-workflow/ using the argo-repo-audit skill. This repo has a WorkflowTemplate.
expectations: Flags the WorkflowTemplate as missing activeDeadlineSeconds — workflows without a deadline can run indefinitely and consume cluster resources
Flags the container steps as missing resource requests and limits
Recommends adding activeDeadlineSeconds at the workflow level and resource limits on each container step
Does NOT invent issues that aren't present in this specific fixture

evals.name: detect-hardcoded-helm-password
evals.prompt: Audit the Argo CD GitOps repository at tests/argo-repo-audit/targeted-bugs/hardcoded-helm-password/ using the argo-repo-audit skill. This repo has one Application that deploys a Redis Helm chart with inline values.
expectations: Detects the hardcoded password in helm.values (auth.password: Redis!Pr0d#2026) as Critical security issue
Explains the risk: password is visible in Git history and in the Application resource on the cluster
Recommends using a valuesFrom Secret reference, Argo CD Vault Plugin, or ExternalSecret instead
Reports overall status as FAIL due to Critical security finding

evals.name: clean-repo-zero-findings
evals.prompt: Audit the Argo CD GitOps repository at tests/argo-repo-audit/clean-repo/ using the argo-repo-audit skill. This repo should follow best practices.
expectations: Reports zero Critical findings
Reports zero Warning findings (or at most Info-level suggestions)
Recognizes the AppProject has properly restricted sourceRepos and destinations (no wildcards)
Recognizes the Application has automated sync with prune, selfHeal, retry, and pinned targetRevision
Recognizes secrets are managed via SealedSecret (not plain-text)
Recognizes the AnalysisTemplate has both successCondition and failureCondition with failureLimit
Recognizes the Rollout has Istio traffic routing configured
Reports overall status as PASS
Does NOT produce false positives — this repo is intentionally clean
